Dr. Alan Hiatt · NWACC · 37 Years · 300,000 Patients
WHAT I
NEEDED
TO HEAR
For every student who ever sat in my classroom and wondered if they were enough.
01
Before anything else
YOU
ALREADY
MATTER
I have watched thousands of students walk through my door unsure of themselves. You have something the world needs. Don't you dare shrink from it.
02
The simplest gift you can give
SHOW
UP
Be on time. For class. For work. For the people who love you. Every time you show up on time you are telling someone — without a single word — that they matter to you.
03
The secret to feeling alive
DO SOMETHING
KIND
TODAY
Hold the door. Pay for the coffee behind you. Your brain releases serotonin and dopamine — you feel it too. Science confirms what your heart already knows: giving is the fastest path to joy.
04
It was never about arrogance
WALK IN
LIKE YOU
BELONG
Confidence is not something you are born with. I have watched quiet, scared students become extraordinary professionals — one small act of courage at a time. Smile first. Make eye contact. Practice it until it becomes your truth.
05
This one changed my life
FIND YOUR
FIVE
PEOPLE
Find five people already living the life you dream about. Get close to them. Watch how they think, how they speak, how they treat others. You will rise to meet them. This is a love story about who you are becoming.
06
I mean this with everything in me
YOUR DREAM
IS NOT
TOO BIG
The dream you are afraid to say out loud — that one. That is the one worth every sacrifice. Dream it. Then dream bigger. Then close your eyes and feel what it feels like when it is real. Your mind will find a way.
07
Nobody told me this early enough
GRIT IS
BUILT
NOT BORN
Write it by hand. Make real flashcards. Draw the diagram. Every time you do the hard thing when you do not feel like it, you are building the person who will not quit when it counts.
08
From someone who went
LEAVE.
GO FAR.
COME BACK.
Go to college in Germany — it is free. Volunteer on a Mercy Ship in Africa. Sit with someone who has nothing and watch them smile anyway. You will come home changed, and that changed version of you will be the greatest gift you ever gave the world.
09
The practice that heals everything
SIT BY
THE WATER
AND BREATHE
Find a lake. A river. A quiet place. Sit there and let the noise fall away. Picture yourself exactly where you want to be. Your brain cannot tell the difference between a vivid vision and reality. Give it something beautiful to aim for.
10
You will not get this time back
LOOK UP
FROM THE
SCREEN
Your grandmother is telling a story right now. Your best friend needs to know you see them. Some of the greatest moments of your life are happening quietly while you scroll. Put the phone down. Be outrageously, completely present.
11
This is what I have learned about love
BUILD
REAL
BONDS
Followers are not friends. Likes are not love. Find the people who show up when things fall apart, who tell you the truth with kindness. Tend those relationships like a garden. They will be the most valuable thing you ever build.
12
Every person I have admired has this
FAIL
FORWARD
ANYWAY
Your failures are not evidence that you were wrong to try. They are the curriculum. The people who change the world are not the ones who never failed. They are the ones who got up one more time than they fell.
13
Still true after 37 years
STAY
CURIOUS
FOREVER
Learn anatomy and also poetry. Study business and also philosophy. The most alive people I have ever met never stopped asking questions. The world is larger and stranger and more beautiful than any one field of study. Be one of them.
14
I am telling you this as your professor
USE THE
TOOLS
OF YOUR TIME
Artificial intelligence is not the future — it is already today. The students who learn to use it now, wisely and thoughtfully, will have an enormous advantage. Use technology for your good. Let it amplify your thinking, not replace it.
15
The most powerful thing I know
YOUR
ATTITUDE
IS YOUR LIFE
I have watched people with every advantage squander it with a bitter heart. And I have watched people with almost nothing build something extraordinary from sheer joy. You cannot always choose what happens to you. You can always choose how you meet it.
From my heart to yours
I BELIEVE
IN YOU.
GO.
I have spent 37 years watching ordinary people do extraordinary things when someone believed in them first. So let me be that person for you. I believe in you. I always have. Now go do something that makes you proud.
With love — Dr. Alan Hiatt · Northwest Arkansas Community College · 2026
